title: Keeping up to date with immigration policy :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Keeping up to date with immigration policy
Immigration New Zealand regularly updates immigration policy. We publish changes online, in the operational manual, and keep immigration advisers and the public up to date.
The rules and processes people must follow before we can give them a visa or permission to enter New Zealand are called 'immigration instructions'. They include requirements an applicant must meet, and evidence they must provide.
These instructions are published in our Operational Manual, which is available online.
We regularly update the Operational Manual to reflect any changes made to government policy and the immigration instructions.
The online manual includes advice on how to use it.
Operational Manual online
Other Operational Manual documents
Upcoming changes to immigration instructions are published in amendment circulars and incorporated into the Operational Manual on the day they come into force.
Policy Amendment Circulars

## Internal administration circulars
Internal administration circulars (IACs) explain internal processes and procedures to immigration staff. We publish IACs when we need to clarify instructions in the Operational Manual.
Internal Administration Circulars (IACs)

## Advice to immigration staff
‘Advice to immigration staff’, or Visa Paks, are regular advice given to staff about how to interpret and apply immigration instructions.
We update advice to reflect best practice as immigration instructions change.
